page: Amazon Web Services c5a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor based on the x86_64 architecture, delivering 64 virtual CPUs across 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The dedicated CPU allocation runs at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance in stress-ng is positioned in the bottom 10%, and Geekbench single-core scores fall into the average range. However, multi-core performance is robust, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores both ranking in the average tier. Notably, the instance demonstrates strong performance in the top 25% for Passmark CPU scores, extended instructions, and encryption tests, alongside strong Geekbench ray tracing and Passmark memory scores. Large-scale LLM text generation is a relative bottleneck, registering in the bottom 25%, while smaller model inference, Redis operations, and static web serving throughput remain average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c5a.16xlarge offers a high density of dedicated AMD EPYC compute cores relative to its memory footprint, making it a viable option for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory-to-CPU ratios or local NVMe storage. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs reduces overhead for purely CPU-bound tasks. This profile is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, cryptography tasks, ray tracing, and general compute-bound batch processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c5a.16xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz. It features 64 vCPUs (32 physical cores), 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, with no bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance exhibits average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, with strong results in Passmark CPU, encryption, and ray tracing benchmarks, though its single-core performance is relatively weak. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for high-density, multi-threaded CPU workloads. It is well-suited for software compilation, cryptography, ray tracing, and general-purpose multi-threaded batch processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC processor, delivering strong multi-core and encryption performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC processors on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A dedicated 64-vCPU compute-optimized platform featuring AMD EPYC processors and DDR4 memory for highly parallelized, multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-core AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz
Equipped with 128 GB of DDR4 memory and 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Strong benchmark performance in cryptography, ray tracing, and Passmark CPU tests
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, optimizing cost for pure compute tasks
Best suited for multi-threaded batch processing, software compilation, and encryption workloads
